q23.explanation: Câu 23 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn 2. Bài nêu: "Finally, the Panama Canal was constructed in two stages. The first between 1881 and 1888, the work being carried out by the French company headed by de Lesseps, and secondly, the work by the Americans, which eventually completed the canal’s construction between 1904 and 1914. The French company ran out of money and an attempt was unsuccessful to raise funds by applying to the French government to issue lottery bonds, which had been successful during the construction of the Suez Canal when that project was at the point of failure through lack of money.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q23.evidence: Finally, the Panama Canal was constructed in two stages. The first between 1881 and 1888, the work being carried out by the French company headed by de Lesseps, and secondly, the work by the Americans, which eventually completed the canal’s construction between 1904 and 1914. The French company ran out of money and an attempt was unsuccessful to raise funds by applying to the French government to issue lottery bonds, which had been successful during the construction of the Suez Canal when that project was at the point of failure through lack of money._

q24.explanation: Câu 24 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 2, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "The Americans proposed that a tidal lock should be constructed at Panama, which solved the problem and reduced excavation by an enormous amount. When construction was finally completed, the Panama Canal ran through various locks and four dams, raising and lowering boats by 26 metres, and running the lengths of two naturally occurring lakes, the 32-mile Gatun Lake and the 5-mile Miraflores Lake.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q24.evidence: The Americans proposed that a tidal lock should be constructed at Panama, which solved the problem and reduced excavation by an enormous amount. When construction was finally completed, the Panama Canal ran through various locks and four dams, raising and lowering boats by 26 metres, and running the lengths of two naturally occurring lakes, the 32-mile Gatun Lake and the 5-mile Miraflores Lake._

q25.explanation: Câu 25 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 1, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "The Panama Canal joins the Pacific and Atlantic oceans while the Suez joins the Red Sea (Indian Ocean) and the Mediterranean (Atlantic Ocean). Both offer ships huge savings in time and mileage. For example, a 9-hour trip on the Panama Canal would save a total of 18,000 miles on a trip from New York to San Francisco.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q25.evidence: The Panama Canal joins the Pacific and Atlantic oceans while the Suez joins the Red Sea (Indian Ocean) and the Mediterranean (Atlantic Ocean). Both offer ships huge savings in time and mileage. For example, a 9-hour trip on the Panama Canal would save a total of 18,000 miles on a trip from New York to San Francisco._

Both offer ships huge savings in time and mileage. For example, a 9-hour trip on the Panama Canal would save a total of 18,000 miles on a trip from New York to San Francisco.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q26.evidence: The Panama Canal joins the Pacific and Atlantic oceans while the Suez joins the Red Sea (Indian Ocean) and the Mediterranean (Atlantic Ocean). q27.explanation: Câu 27 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 2, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "The history of the Panama Canal goes back to the sixteenth century, with a survey of the isthmus and a working plan for a canal ordered by the Spanish government in 1529. In the eighteenth century various companies tried and failed to construct the canal, but it wasn’t until 1880 that a French company, organised by Ferdinand Marie de Lesseps, proposed a sea-level canal through Panama. He believed that if a sea-level canal worked when constructing the Suez Canal, it had to work for the Panama Canal.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q27.evidence: The history of the Panama Canal goes back to the sixteenth century, with a survey of the isthmus and a working plan for a canal ordered by the Spanish government in 1529. In the eighteenth century various companies tried and failed to construct the canal, but it wasn’t until 1880 that a French company, organised by Ferdinand Marie de Lesseps, proposed a sea-level canal through Panama. He believed that if a sea-level canal worked when constructing the Suez Canal, it had to work for the Panama Canal._
